key_insight: A layerwise self-adaptive PINN with dual-phase optimization improves the capture of sharp shock fronts in high-Reynolds multidimensional coupled Burgers' equations.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more stable mesh-free surrogate for shock-dominated transport problems where conventional finite difference and finite element methods struggle with steep gradients.
method: Dynamically reweights physics residual, initial-condition, and boundary-condition losses during training.
Uses a layerwise self-adaptive strategy to improve convergence across the network.
Employs a dual-phase optimization scheme and compares against standard PINN baselines with/without L-BFGS.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments show lower relative L2 error than standard PINNs and better reconstruction of shock-front evolution over time.
watch_item: Performance gains may depend on careful tuning of adaptive weights and optimization phases, especially for stiff, high-Reynolds training cases.
paper_title: MATLAB-Based Layerwise Self-Adaptive Physics-Informed Neural Network in Applications to Multidimensional Coupled Burgers' Equations with High Reynolds Numbers
